title: Yoga, Physical Therapy, or Education for Chronic Low Back Pain: A Randomized Noninferiority Trial
headline: Yoga, Physical Therapy, or Education for Chronic Low Back Pain: A Randomized Noninferiority Trial
summary: A randomized controlled trial of yoga examined clinical research. Yoga is effective for mild to moderate chronic low back pain (cLBP), but its comparative effectiveness with physical therapy (PT) is unknown.
keyFindings: Yoga is effective for mild to moderate chronic low back pain (cLBP), but its comparative effectiveness with physical therapy (PT) is unknown.
Moreover, little is known about yoga's effectiveness in underserved patients with more severe functional disability and pain.
limitations: Participants were not blinded to treatment assignment.
Single-trial result; requires replication, and self-reported outcomes may inflate apparent effects.
The practice studied (yoga) is not the Miracle of Mind practice; findings do not transfer directly to MoM.
simplifiedTitle: Yoga, Physical Therapy, or Education for Chronic Low Back Pain: A Randomized Noninferiority Trial
aiSnippet: RCT, Annals of internal medicine (2017). Yoga is effective for mild to moderate chronic low back pain (cLBP), but its comparative effectiveness with physical therapy (PT) is unknown. Moreover, little is known about yoga's effectiveness in underserved patients with more severe functional disability and pain.
